svg.text: Diagram 1 — What teams build: old vs new
Each row is a layer of work. Red = cost with no domain value. Green = genuine capability.
TODAY — every team builds all of this
React · Next.js · components · pages · routing · state · CSS
Okta / OIDC integration · session management · token refresh
VAPID keys · service workers · subscription management
Pen test · OWASP · secrets audit · auth model review
WCAG 2.2 · screen reader testing · keyboard nav · colour contrast
Who did what, when — usually ad hoc, incomplete, non-compliant
URL · intranet link · tribal knowledge · hoping someone tells you
Docker · k8s · CDN · DNS · SSL · uptime monitoring
The thing only this team can build
→ A2UI renderer + catalog. Platform owned. Zero team effort.
→ Token broker + OBO flow. Declared in manifest. Done.
→ Platform push service. Declare template in manifest.
→ Certified once at platform level. Teams inherit it.
→ WCAG 2.2 AA in every catalog component. Inherited.
→ Concept graph. Natural language. Automatic on xp publish.
→ Platform infrastructure. API only. Team owns their backend.
Domain logic + data + API + domain agent
The thing only this team can build — now with agentic intelligence
This is AOP applied to the organisation. Cross-cutting concerns extracted once. Teams write pure capability.
Diagram 2 — Realistic time to ship a new internal capability
Based on typical enterprise internal tool delivery. Not best case. Not worst case. Representative.
Realistic saving: 12–22 weeks per new capability. Auth, UI, security, a11y — eliminated not accelerated.
Diagram 3 — How Pangaea itself is built agentically
The platform is not a static system. It reasons, learns, and improves. Each component labelled with its mechanism.
① Redis hot-path lookup (deterministic · <50ms · no model)
④ Claude haiku cold-path (structured JSON only)
Fuzzy match → pgvector rank → disambiguation card if ambiguous. Deterministic scoring. Never guesses.
OBO token minted per call · OPA policy check · scopes ⊆ manifest · TTL ≤ 600s. No model. No exceptions.
A2A dispatch · parallel syscalls · partial failure handling · confirm gate
Checkpointed · resumable · consequential actions always gated on human commit
Stream A2UI refs only · cross-domain via syscall
component_id + Zod props · no code · no injection
Run without user session. Triggered by events, schedules, other agents.
no model · auto · <5s after xp publish
weekly: cluster unrouted utterances → Slack digest per team (roadmap signal)
every cold-path success → compiled to Redis → system self-improves
Three agentic growth loops — the system improves automatically
Loop 1 — Hot-path compilation: every cold route → Redis. System gets faster with every interaction. No retraining.
Loop 2 — Concept graph enrichment: every xp publish → concepts embedded → new capability discoverable in <5s. No curation.
Loop 3 — Failed intent signal: unrouted needs → weekly digest → teams build what users asked for → publish → repeat.
A2A — team-owned agents, full domain autonomy
Content/control plane separation enforced at A2UI boundary — typed props only, no code path from domain data to executed logic
